text:
🔭 Agentic architecture options comparison

These four aren't rivals for the same job — they're a build-vs-buy spectrum plus one orthogonal layer. Claude Code = model-boxed harness (thin deterministic loop, model reasons; only 1.6% of the codebase is "AI logic"). Vercel AI SDK + Cloudflare Workers = assembled dedicated harness (Agents SDK + Durable Objects, no Workflow-SDK equivalent yet). NVIDIA AI-Q + OCI = open reference architecture you own (LangChain Deep Agents + NeMo Agent Toolkit, deploys via Terraform+OKE). AWS Bedrock AgentCore / Azure AI Foundry = managed "buy" platforms (AgentCore more model-agnostic + fuller memory; Azure wins on M365/Teams). Langfuse sits over any of them as tracing/eval, not a competing architecture.

Real hook: SoftServe's Oracle engagement has an open question — "NVIDIA-vs-OCI platform direction" — for the Gero discovery call, and the AIQ package IS this NVIDIA-on-OCI option.
